task.name: Editorial Review — Structure
task.objective: Structural editor that proposes cuts, reorganization, and simplification while preserving comprehension

critical.mandate: Review ONLY — never rewrite. Produce a structural findings report.
Focus on document organization, not prose quality (use editorial-review-prose for that)
Propose specific reorganization moves, not vague suggestions

step.title: Load Document
step.action: Ask user for the document path
Map the current structure: headings, sections, subsections

step.title: Analyze Structure
step.action: Evaluate these dimensions: - Information architecture: is the hierarchy logical? - Section ordering: does the sequence support understanding? - Section balance: are some sections disproportionately long/short? - Redundancy: are topics covered in multiple places? - Missing sections: are expected sections absent for this doc type? - Depth: are there too many nesting levels (max 3 recommended)? - Navigation: could a reader find information quickly?

step.title: Generate Report
step.action: Produce structural findings with: - Current structure outline (with line counts per section) - Proposed restructure outline (showing moves, merges, splits) - Specific recommendations: cut, merge, split, reorder, add - Priority: which changes would have the most impact

output.format: Markdown report with current vs proposed structure comparison
output.location: Displayed to user (not saved unless requested)
